title: While & For Loops 🔄
description: Iterate through collections cleanly and safely.

slides.title: The `while` Loop ⏳

quizData.question: What happens if a `while` loop's condition is false the very first time it checks?

options.text: It runs the code block exactly once
It skips the loop block entirely

quizData.explanation: Because a `while` loop checks the condition *before* entering the block, a false condition means the loop is ignored completely.

slides.title: Looping Collections 📚
slides.content:
You *could* theoretically use a `while` loop to scan an array by using an index counter (`a[index]`), but that's error prone (Out of Bounds errors!).

Instead, Rust developers use the **`for`** loop to iterate through collections flawlessly.

slides.title: The `for` Loop 🚀
slides.content: This is entirely safe and incredibly fast.

slides.title: Ranges 🎯

quizData.question: What is printed by `for count in (0..3)`?
quizData.explanation: The range `..` includes the first number, but excludes the terminating number.
